prompt: Should Reveal charge for community access in v1?
method: constitution-bound live-chat simulation (same Nexus packs/contracts/DNA weighting; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unavailable in eval VM)
reply:
No — not for v1, if we mean the founding promise.

Charging at the door recreates the exclusion traditional finance culture already taught her. Belonging before tools means access is part of the story, not a feature flag. Paid later must mean deeper formation or stewardship of a house she already lives in — never buying the right to be safe.

Even if entitlements are easy to code, Storyteller rejects a v1 paywall as a meaning failure. Easy implementation doesn’t make it true.
